rationale: A0 confirms that frida_server — a dynamic instrumentation/hooking tool located in /tmp (non-standard, ephemeral path) — successfully attached via ptrace to QGroundControl (a UAV ground control application) with root effective privileges, indicating privilege escalation and process injection into a safety-critical control process. Within ~61 seconds of that attachment, EV-001 shows a COMPONENT_ARM_DISARM command was accepted by vehicle-01, EV-002 confirms the vehicle transitioned to a disarmed state (motors cut), and EV-003 records a ground impact at ~19.4 m/s (~70 km/h), consistent with an in-flight disarm causing an uncontrolled crash. The temporal sequence — ptrace injection into QGroundControl followed immediately by an anomalous disarm command and physical impact — strongly indicates the frida_server was used to manipulate QGroundControl's MAVLink command pipeline to issue the disarm, resulting in a confirmed physical impact. No benign explanation accounts for a /tmp-resident frida_server attaching to a flight control application immediately preceding a fatal vehicle disarm event.
